verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is synthesized as a type I single-pass transmembrane proligand (169 aa; 21-aa TM domain confirmed). Surface biotinylation confirms the TM precursor transiently reaches the plasma membrane (>90% basolateral in polarized epithelial cells). However, ADAM metalloprotease-mediated ectodomain shedding rapidly releases the 46-aa mature EGF-like domain as the dominant soluble signaling actor. The TM-anchored stage is real but short-lived and not the canonical cell-surface form — the shed soluble ectodomain drives downstream EGFR/ERBB4 biology. This fits the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern: contextual/dual_localization.
